title: Municipal Solid Waste Collection Gaps And Open Dumping Health Risk In South Asian Cities
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring municipal solid waste collection gaps and open-dumping health risk in South Asian cities, linking waste-generation estimates, collection-route data, and informal-sector recycling data at neighborhood scale.
why_it_matters: South Asian cities generate over 200 million tons of solid waste annually, with collection rates below 60 percent in most municipalities. Open dumping and burning of uncollected waste cause respiratory disease, groundwater contamination, and methane emissions. Informal waste pickers handle 15-30 percent of recycling but are invisible in official statistics. Neighborhood-scale waste-collection gap mapping is nearly absent from the most affected cities.

success_metrics.target: Every collection-gap estimate links waste-generation, collection-route, and informal-sector data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
